questions.scenario: You bought shares at $100. The stock dropped to $60, then recovered to $85. Fundamentals suggest fair value is $80. Your original $100 purchase price keeps influencing your thinking.
questions.question_text: At what price would you sell the stock?

choices.text: Sell now at $85 -- take the loss and move on to better opportunities
Hold until $100 -- you need to at least break even
Hold until $120 -- you deserve a profit for enduring the drawdown
Buy more at $85 since fair value is $80 -- the anchoring to $100 is irrelevant

questions.behavioral_insight: Tests anchoring to purchase price: can investor evaluate based on current fundamentals

questions.scenario: A viral social media trend is driving a 40% rally in a speculative asset class. Your colleagues and friends are all investing. Financial news covers it 24/7. The asset has no fundamental backing.
questions.question_text: How do you respond to this market frenzy?

choices.text: Ignore it completely -- speculative assets don't belong in a serious portfolio
Research it thoroughly, then allocate a small 2-3% position if fundamentals support it
Invest 10% of your portfolio -- there's clearly momentum here
Go all-in on the trend -- you don't want to miss the next big thing

questions.behavioral_insight: Tests susceptibility to herd behavior and FOMO under social pressure

questions.scenario: Breaking news: A major geopolitical event causes markets to drop 5% in 30 minutes. Your broker calls and says you have 10 minutes to decide before the next circuit breaker halts trading.
questions.question_text: With only 10 minutes to decide, what do you do?

choices.text: Sell everything before the circuit breaker hits -- protect what's left
Sell half your positions to reduce exposure while keeping some upside
Do nothing -- refuse to make decisions under time pressure
Place limit buy orders at 8% below current prices to catch the dip

questions.behavioral_insight: Tests decision quality under extreme time pressure and panic conditions

questions.scenario: Markets have been rallying for 6 months with the S&P 500 up 25%. Valuations are stretched at 22x earnings. Your portfolio is heavily in tech stocks that drove most of the gains.
questions.question_text: How do you position your portfolio for the next 12 months?

choices.text: Rotate entirely into value stocks and bonds -- this rally can't continue
Rebalance to reduce tech concentration and add defensive sectors
Hold current positions -- momentum is your friend and trends persist
Add more to the winners -- the best performers will keep outperforming

questions.behavioral_insight: Tests recency bias: overweighting recent performance in forward-looking decisions

market_calibration.calibration_notes: Market regime: bull_low_vol; LOW STRESS: Questions focus on preference discovery and subtle biases

contradiction_flags.description: User showed aggressive behavior in anchoring scenario but very conservative in recency_bias -- risk signal gap of 65%
User showed growth-oriented behavior in herd_behavior scenario but very conservative in recency_bias -- risk signal gap of 55%

detected_biases.evidence: User heavily weights recent market events in decisions (recency risk signal: 0.15)

llm_narrative.investor_narrative: Quantitative analysis indicates a moderate_volatile investor profile with risk score 0.37.
